objectTags.name: is_player
objectTags.description: Object is part of the resolved Player role.
objectTags.specification: An object has is_player when it is the object named by the Player object, Player synonym, or a member of the Player property in the compiled game. If Player resolves to a property with multiple member objects, every member object has is_player true.

objectTags.name: is_background
objectTags.description: Object is part of the resolved Background role.
objectTags.specification: An object has is_background when it is the object named by the Background object, Background synonym, or a member of the Background property in the compiled game. If Background resolves to a property with multiple member objects on the same collision layer, every member object has is_background true.

objectTags.name: level_presence
objectTags.description: Whether the object appears in all, some, or no playable levels.
objectTags.specification: The level_presence tag is one of all, some, or none. Message levels are ignored. The value all means there is at least one playable level and the object appears in every playable level. The value some means the object appears in at least one but not every playable level. The value none means the object appears in no playable levels; if a valid source has zero playable levels, every object has level_presence none.

objectTags.name: created_by_rules
objectTags.description: A solver-active rule may write this object present in a cell.
objectTags.specification: An object has created_by_rules when rule object-write analysis finds a solver-active rule that may write that object present in a cell where the left-hand side does not already require that object to be present in the same cell. This is a cell-local write tag: relocation can count as creation in the destination cell, even if the object's total count is preserved.

objectTags.name: destroyed_by_rules
objectTags.description: A solver-active rule may write this object absent from a cell.
objectTags.specification: An object has destroyed_by_rules when rule object-write analysis finds a solver-active rule that may write that object absent from a cell where the left-hand side allows that object to be present in the same cell. This includes explicit absent writes, removing a left-hand side object from its original cell, and overwriting a possible same-layer occupant. This is a cell-local write tag: relocation can count as destruction in the source cell, even if the object's total count is preserved.

ruleTags.name: objects_required
ruleTags.description: Concrete objects definitely required present by the rule LHS.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has objects_required entries for concrete objects whose presence is definitely required by positive LHS terms. Single-object terms are required. Objects of required aggregates are all tagged as required. Members of an OR property are not individually required because any one member may satisfy the term.

ruleTags.name: objects_matched
ruleTags.description: Concrete objects whose presence may satisfy a positive LHS term.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has objects_matched entries for every concrete object that may satisfy a positive LHS term after resolving synonyms, properties, and aggregates to object names. This includes concrete required objects, OR-property alternatives, and objects in aggregates.

ruleTags.name: object_absences_matched
ruleTags.description: Concrete objects whose absence may satisfy a negative LHS term.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has object_absences_matched entries for every concrete object that may satisfy a negative LHS term after resolving synonyms, properties, and aggregates to object names. For example, no Obstacle records every concrete object in Obstacle.

ruleTags.name: movements_required
ruleTags.description: Concrete object movements definitely required by the rule LHS.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has movements_required entries of the form Object:movement for concrete object movement terms that the LHS definitely requires. Single-object movement terms are required. Members of an OR property are not individually required because any one member may satisfy the movement term.

ruleTags.name: movements_matched
ruleTags.description: Concrete object movements whose presence may satisfy a rule LHS movement term.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has movements_matched entries of the form Object:movement for every concrete object movement that may satisfy a positive LHS movement term after resolving synonyms, properties, and aggregates to object names. Property movement terms are expanded to their concrete members as may-matches.

ruleTags.name: objects_written
ruleTags.description: Concrete objects written present by the rule RHS.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has objects_written entries for concrete objects the RHS may write present in a cell where the LHS does not already require that object present in the same cell. This is cell-local: relocation writes the object at the destination even if total object count is preserved.

ruleTags.name: objects_erased
ruleTags.description: Concrete objects written absent by the rule RHS.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has objects_erased entries for concrete objects the RHS may write absent from a cell where the LHS allows that object to be present in the same cell. This includes explicit no terms, removal from the original cell, and possible same-layer erasure caused by writing another object on the layer.

ruleTags.name: movements_written
ruleTags.description: Concrete object movements written or cleared by the rule RHS.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has movements_written entries of the form Object:movement for concrete object movements the RHS may write in a cell where the corresponding LHS cell does not already have the same Object:movement. RHS movement terms use the compiled movement name visible to this analysis stage, after resolving synonyms, properties, and aggregates to object names. If an LHS movement requirement is removed while the object remains present in the corresponding RHS cell with no RHS movement, the rule writes Object:stationary.

ruleTags.name: movements_removed
ruleTags.description: Concrete object movements no longer present after the rule RHS.
ruleTags.specification: A rule has movements_removed entries of the form Object:movement for concrete LHS movement matches that are not preserved by an identical RHS movement match for the same object in the corresponding RHS cell. This includes direction changes, movement clears, object relocation, object deletion, and action matches consumed by an RHS movement write.

factFamilies.name: program_flow
factFamilies.description: Pairwise wake graph: which rules may, when applied, create a new match for which other rules.
factFamilies.specification: The program_flow fact lists, for the whole program, every ordered rule pair (R1, R2) where R1's RHS writes may enable a new match for R2's LHS. An edge is emitted whenever R1's writes intersect R2's reads in any of: object presence (writing an object R2 requires present), object absence (erasing an object R2 requires absent), or movement (writing an object:movement R2 reads, including the cardinal-to-moving subsumption). Edges are listed for all ordered pairs across all rule sections without scope segmentation; self-edges (R -> R) are kept when present. The again_rules field lists every rule whose firing semantically forces a tick restart (rules whose summary commands include `again`). Consumers that want the worst-case wake set should take the union of wake_edges with again_rules x rule_ids; this expansion is intentionally not materialised in the fact.

winConditionTags.name: objects_matched
winConditionTags.description: Concrete objects whose presence or absence is read by this win condition.
winConditionTags.specification: A win condition has objects_matched entries for every concrete object referenced in either the subjects mask or the targets mask, after resolving properties to concrete names. This is the conservative union: an object appears here whenever its distribution on the board could affect the win outcome, regardless of quantifier. objects_matched = subjects_matched ∪ targets_matched ∪ object_absences_matched.

winConditionTags.name: subjects_matched
winConditionTags.description: Concrete objects in the subjects mask when the quantifier is SOME or ALL.
winConditionTags.specification: A win condition has subjects_matched entries for the concrete objects in the subjects mask when the quantifier is SOME (0) or ALL (1). When the quantifier is NO (-1) the subjects represent an absence requirement; in that case subjects_matched is empty and the same concrete names appear in object_absences_matched instead.

winConditionTags.name: targets_matched
winConditionTags.description: Concrete objects in the targets mask (the 'on X' part of a win condition).
winConditionTags.specification: A win condition has targets_matched entries for the concrete objects in the targets mask, after resolving properties to concrete names. The targets mask is the optional second mask in a two-mask win condition of the form 'Some X on Y'. When there is no targets mask, targets_matched is empty. The targets mask applies to all quantifiers.

winConditionTags.name: object_absences_matched
winConditionTags.description: Concrete objects whose absence is the win signal when the quantifier is NO.
winConditionTags.specification: A win condition has object_absences_matched entries for the concrete objects in the subjects mask when the quantifier is NO (-1). Under NO, the win condition is satisfied when none of those objects are present, so the subjects mask represents an absence read rather than a presence read. When the quantifier is SOME or ALL, object_absences_matched is empty.
